message:
perf(ingest): bake ephemeral-writer pragmas into open path

Last commit's perf cleanup removed the per-chunk preflight write but
still left the rest of the ephemeral-writer setup as a sequence of
post-open PRAGMA overrides. On every short-lived writer
`with_ephemeral_writer` was:

1. Calling `FrankenStorage::open_writer(...)` — which internally ran
   the full default `apply_config()` (FK=ON, busy_timeout=5000,
   wal_autocheckpoint=1000).
2. Then calling `apply_index_writer_busy_timeout(&writer)` to bump
   busy_timeout to whatever the index writer wants.
3. Then calling `apply_index_writer_checkpoint_policy(&writer,
   defer_checkpoints)` to override `wal_autocheckpoint`.
4. Then running `PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF` directly on the raw
   handle to disable FK enforcement (CASS #169).

That's three round-trips that immediately undo what `apply_config()`
just set, on a connection that's about to do a short-lived write
and then close. Each PRAGMA crosses the FFI boundary, so doing them
twice on every persist costs visible time in the steady state.

Storage layer (`src/storage/sqlite.rs`):

- Promote three magic numbers to file-scope constants:
  `DEFAULT_CACHE_SIZE_KIB = 65_536`,
  `DEFAULT_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000`,
  `DEFAULT_WAL_AUTOCHECKPOINT_PAGES = 1000`.
- Split `apply_config()` into a thin wrapper around a new
  `apply_writable_config(busy_timeout_ms, wal_autocheckpoint_pages,
  foreign_keys_enabled)`. The general-purpose config keeps the same
  shape (FK=ON, 5s timeout, 1000-page autocheckpoint) by passing
  the three constants. The format strings now use the parameter
  values, so a different opener can dial them in without a follow-up
  PRAGMA.
- Add `FrankenStorage::open_ephemeral_writer(path, busy_timeout_ms,
  wal_autocheckpoint_pages)`. Opens the underlying connection,
  wraps it via `Self::new(...)`, then calls
  `apply_writable_config(..., false)` so foreign keys are OFF from
  the very first `apply_config` step instead of being toggled OFF
  after the fact. CASS #169 motivation moves into the
  `apply_writable_config` doc comment so future readers see why
  the default is split into FK-on and FK-off variants.

Indexer (`src/indexer/mod.rs`):

- `with_ephemeral_writer` now reads the writer-knob values once via
  `index_writer_busy_timeout_ms()` and
  `index_writer_wal_autocheckpoint_pages(defer_checkpoints)` before
  opening the writer, then opens with
  `FrankenStorage::open_ephemeral_writer(&db_path, busy_timeout_ms,
  wal_autocheckpoint_pages)`. The post-open
  `apply_index_writer_busy_timeout(&writer)`,
  `apply_index_writer_checkpoint_policy(&writer, defer_checkpoints)`,
  and direct `PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF` calls are deleted — they
  were paying for the same configuration twice. The
  `apply_index_writer_checkpoint_policy(storage, defer_checkpoints)`
  call on the long-lived handle is kept (it was specifically
  documented as keeping the long-lived observability state aligned
  with the short-lived writer).
- The single-conversation `persist_conversation` path moves the
  `let internal_conv = map_to_internal(conv);` and the corresponding
  `Agent { ... }` build back inside the
  `with_ephemeral_writer(..., |writer| { ... })` closure where they
  were originally. The previous "do it outside the closure" framing
  doesn't survive the new shape — the busy-timeout and
  wal-autocheckpoint reads now live just before the closure, so
  pulling everything else out crowded the open site without
  measurable benefit.

Test (`open_ephemeral_writer_applies_specialized_writer_pragmas`):
- Open a fresh DB, drop, reopen via `open_ephemeral_writer(..., 60_000, 0)`.
- Assert `PRAGMA foreign_keys` returns 0, `PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint`
  returns 0, and `PRAGMA busy_timeout` returns 60000. This pins both
  the new FK-off behavior and that the open-time `busy_timeout_ms`
  / `wal_autocheckpoint_pages` arguments actually flow through
  `apply_writable_config`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
